title: [libartifact] Introduce an event channel in `ArtifactStore`
body:
#### Proposal
I propose adding an event channel to the [ArtifactStore](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/common/pkg/libartifact/store/store.go#L50) struct 


#### Motivation
The primary goal is to provide a mechanism for `ArtifactStore` to broadcast events related to its operations, such as artifact `push` or `pull` actions.
It is an issue in podman https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27260#issuecomment-3475339813

#### Benefits
This addition offers two significant advantages:
1. Enables Real-time Event Consumption: Clients using `ArtifactStore` can subscribe to this channel. This allows them to receive immediate notifications and react to artifact events as they happen.

2. Decouples Event Logic: This design simplifies the client's responsibilities. Clients are decoupled from the specifics of event generation. They can simply consume events directly from the provided channel.

#### Reference
This is the same architecture as `libmage.Runtime`
- runtime channel: https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/common/libimage/runtime.go#L70
- runtime client: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2e738b9b871f98930d34ca97bc0eb19a324193ea/libpod/runtime.go#L708

comments.body: @nimdrak are yous till wanting to do this?
@baude Yes, I'm still interested! I'd be happy to work on this if it's still needed.

I (we) would *love* to see this done.  I was reading the issue that pointed me here from podman and i agree with the architecture proposal.  I see this is step 1,  and then step 2 is in podman?  If you need any help, make sure you hang out in `podman-dev` on matrix as we are there too.

Yes, that's correct. this is step 1, and the follow-up will be in Podman. 
I'll share the step 1 code and a detailed explanation shortly. Thanks for the support!

I would like to get your feedback on my proposed approach for implementing event handling in the artifact store. I plan to follow the same pattern used in [common/libimage/runtime.go](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/c631fbe2c694c21ccac297dc6f7a67b9f9a63459/common/libimage/runtime.go#L70)

Here is my high-level plan:

**Step 1 (libartifact/store)**
1. Add an event channel field and related initialization methods to `ArtifactStore`.
2. Create `libartifact/store/events.go` to define the event structure and types.
3. Add the logic to dispatch these events within `libartifact/store/store.go`.

**Step 2 (podman)**
1. Update `libpod/events/config.go` to include standard Podman events for Artifacts (e.g., `Artifact Type = "artifact"`).
2. Create an `r.artifactEvents()` goroutine in `libpod/runtime.go` (to be called around the same time `ArtifactStore` is initialized).
3. Translate artifact events into standard Podman event logs.

Does this sound like the right direction? If you'd prefer, I can open a draft PR to show you the code.
[Matrix link](https://matrix.to/#/!SEjxdEhDPWoPxzjWCX:matrix.org/$c5cWU_PUnS4-F0ti5LcO8uwB5MkjkUjV3-Dy9tt9Vv4?via=holzinger.dev&via=t2bot.io&via=matrix.org)

> Does this sound like the right direction? If you'd prefer, I can open a draft PR to show you the code.

It certainly does to me.  how about you @mheon ?
Yeah, this plan SGTM - it's how I'd implement myself

> Add an event channel field and related initialization methods to ArtifactStore.

I do not quite like a channel approach. I know this is what libimage does but it has caused some problems for podman, the extra goroutine caused event ordering issues with podman which causes real world test issues, https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23634.

I think a better design would be to move the types of libpod/events/config.go here into common/pkg/events or something. Then the store here should accept an EventWrite interface or something were we can submit the event in place directly.

We could then switch libimage to do the same to fix these order issues. It would also be more efficient as we can aviod the extra channels/goroutine work.
@Luap99 should we take the contributions as is and then do that all in one sweep?
I would be ok with that.

I see your point. Moving the event types to common and using an interface instead of channels definitely sounds like a cleaner and more robust approach.

As baude suggested, I'll proceed with the current plan for this contribution first, and then we can look into the refactoring. 

I’ll start working on the code as proposed and share it shortly. Thanks for the feedback!
